calendar.matters:
Notice Of Plaintiff'S Amended Unopposed Motion And Motion For Dismissal Pursuant To Califorina Rules Of Court Rule 3770(A)-(C); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Prior to the hearing, Counsel appeared to inform the court a proposed order was submitted via email. Clerk informs counsel if the proposed order is verbatim it should be signed after the hearing and Counsel can retrieve a copy for the court's website.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Jonathan Rubin's unopposed Amended Motion For Dismissal Of Class And Representative Allegations Pursuant To California Rules Of Court Rule 3[.]770(A)-(C) is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing and consistent with California Rules of Court, rule 3.770, the court orders Plaintiff may dismiss the class allegations and claims against Defendant Properfood, LLC without prejudice and dismiss his individual claims against Defendant with prejudice. The record demonstrates that dismissal will not prejudice class members, thus no notice to the class is required.

After this executed order is filed in the ROA, Plaintiff must file a request for dismissal on CIV-110.

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order quoting the above verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION / Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Class And Representative Allegations Pursuant To California Rules Of Court Rule 3770(A)-(C)
Off Calendar Mar-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Prior to the hearing counsel sent an email to clarify the ruling of the court. At 8:48am the court receives an email that no one is contesting the court's ruling and there will be no appearances.

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Jonathan Rubin's unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Class And Representative Allegations Pursuant To California Rules Of Court Rule 3770(A)-(C) is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Plaintiff seeks a court order under California Rules of Court rule 3.770. Plaintiff does not provide sufficient information or analysis. "Requests for dismissal must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth the facts on which the party relies. The declaration must clearly state whether consideration, direct or indirect, is being given for the dismissal and must describe the consideration in detail." (California Rules of Court, rule 3.770(a).) The Carlin Declaration does not describe the consideration given at all, let alone "in detail." Nor does it clearly state whether consideration, direct or indirect, is being given for dismissal of the class claims (even if without prejudice). Nor is there any analysis in the memorandum of points and authorities demonstrating Plaintiffs received no consideration for dismissal of the class claims, which generally will require an analysis of the individual claims, their value, the settlement amount, the relation between these figures; the attorney's fees recovery and whether it demonstrates consideration for abandoning the class claims. The court's adjudication will likely focus on the specific terms of the settlement agreement; at least the relevant provisions must be before the court and part of the public record on the motion. The court is not inclined to review the settlement without public access even if the parties intended to keep it confidential.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION / Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Class And Representative Allegations Pursuant To California Rules Of Court Rule 3770(A)-(C)
